findings.evidence: `rg` shows line 97 proves `oddPrimeDiscriminant_emod_four {p : ℕ} (hp : Odd p)`, while line 109 adds `oddPrimeDiscriminant_emod_four_of_prime {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp2 : p ≠ 2)` and no other file references the specialized name.
findings.fix: Remove this public special case, or keep only the general signature `theorem oddPrimeDiscriminant_emod_four {p : ℕ} (hp : Odd p) : oddPrimeDiscriminant p % 4 = 1` and let users derive `Odd p` from primality at call sites.
findings.issue: `oddPrimeDiscriminant_emod_four_of_prime` has stronger assumptions than the nearby general theorem `oddPrimeDiscriminant_emod_four`; it requires `p.Prime` and `p ≠ 2` only to recover `Odd p`.

summary: The public API includes a prime-specific duplicate of an immediately preceding odd-number theorem. That specialization is not used elsewhere in the PR and sits below the natural Mathlib generality level for this fact.
